(0.03-0.6 mg/kg/hr) • Dilute 5 amp in NS 100 ml (2 mg/ml), run 7.5 ml/hr with titrate. (0.18-0.96 mg/kg/hr) Indicator of depth of NM blockade: • Clinical signs: spontaneous ventilation, eye opening, sustained hand grip, leg lift. • Peripheral nerve stimulator: train of four (inaccurate under edema or hypothermia). General concern：muscle weakness, corneal abrasion (Rx. lubricating eye drops), ICU acquired weakness (high risk in patients with concurrent steroid use and poor sugar control). NEUROMUSCULAR BLOCKADE Patients need adequate pain control and deep sedation (RASS -4~-5). Nimbex (cisatracurium 2 mg/ml, 5 ml) (NT$ 73/vial) Indication • Emergent intubation • Ventilator dys-synchrony • Therapeutic hypothermia • Status asthmaticus • Increased intra-cranial pressure • Increased intra-abdominal pressure restart infusion at 50% of prior rate restart infusion at 50% of prior rate • Pure infusion (2 mg/ml), run 1.5 ml/hr with titrate. (0.03-0.6 mg/kg/hr) Target under-depth over-depth restart infusion at 50% of prior rate ↑ 0.5 ml/hr every 30 mins • Pure infusion (10 mg/ml), run 1.5 ml/hr with titrate. (0.18-0.96 mg/kg/hr) ↑ 0.5 ml/hr every 60 mins hold infusion Target under-depth over-depth hold infusion restart infusion at 50% of prior rate 1st CHOICEl allergy or Nimbex unavailable

ANALGESIA  Reducing precipitating medication (BZDs, Z-drugs, opioids, anti-cholinergics, diphenhydramine) and blood transfusion.  Optimizing sleep.  Early mobilization.  Hearing and vision aids. Assess pain score prn and Q4-8H, then adjust dosage to reach target. Bolus 0.5-1 ml prn or every 4 hrs  EPS, hypotension, and QTc prolongation.  Onset: 5-30 mins; duration: 2-6 hrs; high protein binding.  Alternatives: quetiapine (Seroquel PO) 12.5-50 mg q12-24h, olanzapine (Zyprexa PO) 2.5-10 mg qd, ziprasidone (Geodon PO) 20-40 mg q12h. YES PAIN morphine allergy ? or hemodynamic instability ? or renal of hepatic insufficiency ? NO  Acetaminophen (Acetamol 1 g IV) (Acetal 500 mg PO) q6h.  Ketorolac (Keto 30 mg IV/IM) stat, then q6h up to < 5 d, if no bleeding risk, ulcer, or renal insufficiency.  Pregabalin (Lyrica 75 mg PO) bid, max. 600 mg, for neuropathic pain. General concern：constipation (Rx. laxatives), nausea/vomiting (Rx. prokinetic agents), delirium, urinary retention, respiratory depression. *NRS = Numeric Rating Scale; BPS = Behavioral Pain Scale; CPOT = Critical-care Pain Observation Tool.
